amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The mating response in S. cerevisiae is a well-characterized MAPK signaling cascade that enables haploid cells of opposite mating types (a and α) to communicate, polarize, and fuse. The process begins when mating pheromones (a-factor or α-factor) bind to G protein-coupled receptors, triggering dissociation of the heterotrimeric G protein. The freed Gβγ subunits activate a three-tiered MAPK cascade (Ste11-Ste7-Fus3/Kss1) that phosphorylates transcription factors, inducing mating-specific genes. Cells arrest in G1, form mating projections (shmoos), and undergo cell and nuclear fusion. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from pheromone sensing through signal transduction, transcriptional response, morphological changes, and cell fusion.

graph TD
    A[Mating Partner Proximity] -->|a-cell Secretes| B[a-factor Pheromone]
    A -->|α-cell Secretes| C[α-factor Pheromone]
    
    B --> D[Ste3 Receptor on α-cell]
    C --> E[Ste2 Receptor on a-cell]
    
    D --> F{GPCR Activation?}
    E --> F
    F -->|Pheromone Bound| G[Conformational Change]
    F -->|No Pheromone| H[Basal State]
    
    G --> I[Gpa1 Gβγ Heterotrimer]
    I --> J{G Protein Dissociation?}
    J -->|GTP Exchange| K[Gpa1-GTP Release]
    J -->|GDP Bound| L[\Inactive Complex/]
    
    K --> M[Free Gβγ Ste4-Ste18]
    M --> N{Downstream Signaling?}
    
    N -->|MAPK Cascade| O[Ste5 Scaffold Recruitment]
    N -->|Polarity| P[Far1 Cdc24 Recruitment]
    N -->|Transcription| Q[Direct Effects]
    
    O --> R[Ste5 Scaffold Complex]
    R --> S{MAPK Tier 1?}
    S -->|MAPKKK| T[Ste11 Activation]
    
    T --> U[Ste50 Adaptor]
    U --> V[Ste11 Autophosphorylation]
    V --> W{MAPK Tier 2?}
    W -->|MAPKK| X[Ste7 Phosphorylation]
    
    X --> Y{MAPK Tier 3?}
    Y -->|Fus3| Z[Fus3 Activation]
    Y -->|Kss1| AA[Kss1 Activation]
    
    Z --> AB{Fus3 Substrates?}
    AB -->|Far1| AC[Far1 Phosphorylation]
    AB -->|Ste12| AD[Ste12 Activation]
    AB -->|Dig1 Dig2| AE[/Repressor Phosphorylation/]
    
    AC --> AF[/Cln-CDK Inhibition/]
    AF --> AG[G1 Cell Cycle Arrest]
    
    AD --> AH{Transcription Factors?}
    AE --> AH
    AH -->|Ste12 Active| AI[Pheromone Response Elements]
    AH -->|Dig1/2 Inactivated| AJ[/Derepression/]
    
    AI --> AK{Mating Gene Expression?}
    AJ --> AK
    AK -->|FUS Genes| AL[/Cell Fusion Machinery/]
    AK -->|FIG Genes| AM[Mating Projection]
    AK -->|Pheromone| AN[Increased Pheromone]
    AK -->|Agglutinins| AO[Cell Adhesion]
    
    P --> AP[Cdc24 GEF Recruitment]
    AP --> AQ[Cdc42 Activation]
    AQ --> AR{Polarity Establishment?}
    
    AR -->|Actin| AS[Actin Cable Formation]
    AR -->|Secretion| AT[Polarized Exocytosis]
    AR -->|Morphology| AU[Shmoo Formation]
    
    AS --> AV[Bni1 Formin]
    AV --> AW[Actin Polymerization]
    AW --> AT
    
    AT --> AX[Vesicle Delivery to Tip]
    AX --> AU
    
    AU --> AY{Mating Projection Growth?}
    AY -->|Continuous| AZ[Elongated Shmoo]
    AY -->|Stalled| BA[Projection Maintenance]
    
    AZ --> BB{Partner Contact?}
    BB -->|Yes| BC[Cell-Cell Recognition]
    BB -->|No| AY
    
    BC --> BD[Agglutinin Binding]
    BD --> BE{Cell Adhesion?}
    BE -->|Aga1-Aga2 α-cell| BF[Strong Attachment]
    BE -->|Sag1 a-cell| BF
    
    BF --> BG[/Cell Wall Degradation?/]
    BG -->|Fus1 Fus2| BH[Local Wall Remodeling]
    BH --> BI[Membrane Exposure]
    
    BI --> BJ{Membrane Fusion?}
    BJ -->|Prm1 Fusion Protein| BK[Plasma Membrane Fusion]
    BK --> BL[Cytoplasmic Mixing]
    
    BL --> BM{Nuclear Migration?}
    BM -->|Kar3 Motor| BN[Nuclear Congression]
    BN --> BO{Nuclear Fusion?}
    
    BO -->|Kar5 Kar8| BP[Outer Membrane Fusion]
    BP --> BQ[Prm3 Fus2]
    BQ --> BR[Inner Membrane Fusion]
    BR --> BS(\1)
    
    BS --> BT[Diploid Nucleus]
    BT --> BU[Diploid Cell]
    
    BU --> BV{Cell Fate?}
    BV -->|Nutrient Rich| BW[Diploid Growth]
    BV -->|Nutrient Poor| BX[Sporulation Program]
    
    M --> BY{Negative Regulation?}
    BY -->|Sst2 RGS| BZ[GTPase Acceleration]
    BZ --> CA(\1)
    CA --> CB[/Signal Termination/]
    
    BY -->|Msg5 Phosphatase| CC[MAPK Dephosphorylation]
    CC --> CB
    
    BY -->|Bar1 Protease a-cell| CD[/α-factor Degradation/]
    CD --> CB

name: Yeast Mating Response
notes: Created based on extensive research on S. cerevisiae mating, the paradigm for GPCR signaling and MAPK cascades in eukaryotes.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive peer-reviewed literature on yeast mating, including studies on pheromone signaling, MAPK cascades, cell polarity, and cell fusion. The pathway components, regulatory interactions, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. The yeast mating response serves as a paradigm for GPCR signaling and MAPK cascades in all eukaryotes.

sources.title: Pheromone signaling mechanisms in yeast: a prototypical sex machine
The yeast pheromone response pathway: a paradigm for eukaryotic signal transduction
Cell fusion during yeast mating requires high levels of a-factor mating pheromone
Mechanisms and regulation of mating-type switching in Saccharomyces cerevisiae

verifiedBy: Manual creation with PubMed literature review
